response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily which made his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David collected three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together Emily David and Sophia collected 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. This totals 76 blueberries.

***

They used all their blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins required 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make they divided the total number of blueberries by 9. This calculation gave them 8 batches with some blueberries left over. They sold each batch for $5. Multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch gave them the total amount of money they made.

***

The calculation for the total money made was straightforward. They had 8 batches and each batch sold for $5. Multiplying these numbers together gave them $40. This was the total amount of money they earned from selling the muffins. They used all their collected blueberries to make the muffins and sold them at a fixed price per batch.

***

The money they made from the blueberries they collected was $40. This amount represents the earnings from their muffin sales. The process involved collecting blueberries making muffins and selling them. Their efforts resulted in a total of $40 which they earned through their hard work. The youth of the group was evident in their determination and teamwork.
